address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

msContents.summary: A large miscellany titled "من آثار البيان" ("From the Writings of the Bayán") at its head, containing selections from the Báb's writings "of almost every variety": prayers, forms of visitation, letters to Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel and other believers, and extracts from the Commentary on the Súra-i-Yúsuf. Browne enumerates and briefly characterizes roughly 65 individual pieces.

msItem.title: Min āthār al-bayān
من آثار البيان
From the Writings of the Bayán (miscellany of prayers, visitations, and letters)
msItem.note: Pieces 1-15 (ff. 2b-76a) fall into three groups of five, each illustrating the "five grades" described under BBF. 2.
Piece 17 (ff. 78b-89a) is an epistle Browne identifies, on internal evidence, as addressed to a Bábí named Muḥammad 'Alí - probably Mullá Muḥammad 'Alí of Zanján, titled Jenáb-i-Ḥujjat ("the Proof").
Piece 18 (ff. 82b-93a) is an epistle to "one of [the Báb's] chief apostles," containing (f. 84a) a notable passage asserting the Báb's identity with all previous and future prophets.

Piece 23 (ff. 98a-107a), headed "فى زيارة الشهداء فى طبرسى," is the form of prayer ordained by the Báb for the visitation of Sheykh Ṭabarsí and commemoration of the martyrs who fell there, including a prayer addressed to Mullá Ḥuseyn of Bushraweyh, quoted at length by Browne. Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel told Browne this form of visitation was composed by the Báb himself.
Piece 46 is the document (published and translated by Browne at pp. 996-7 of his B. ii) in which Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel is designated the Báb's successor.
Piece 52 is the same text as the first piece in BBF. 1. Piece 53 (ff. 150a-153a) is the 57th chapter of the Commentary on the Súra-i-Yúsuf (see Browne's article, pp. 261-8).

Piece 58 is an extract from the Kitábu'l-Asmá' with ordinances on the Bábí calendar (the year explicitly described as 361 days, 19x19, with no intercalary days - Browne infers the Bahá'í intercalary days were a later introduction). A specimen date given in the text places its writing in the last year of the Báb's life (October 1849).

Piece 59 is a letter of instructions to Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel. Browne quotes (f. 175a-b) a passage he considers important as showing the Báb regarded Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel as his sole vicegerent and did not anticipate "Him whom God shall manifest" appearing in Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel's own lifetime; and (f. 175b) a further passage showing the Báb intentionally left the Persian Bayán incomplete (publishing 11 of 19 Váḥids), conferring on Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel the right to complete it - which Browne notes corroborates Comte de Gobineau's account. A related passage (f. 175b) refers to seven Váḥids sent to Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel for distribution to believers in six named provinces (Fárs, 'Iráq, Khurásán, Ádharbáyján, Mázandarán, and Kirmán), the intended recipients being identified only by cipher-names; Browne quotes a letter from Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel (18 March 1892) glossing several of these names (e.g. Ismu'l-Anís = Ḥájí Suleymán Khán, martyred Tehran 1852; Ismu'l-Ḥujjat = Mullá Muḥammad 'Alí of Zanján; Ismu'l-Ṣádiq = Mullá Muḥammad Ṣádiq, "Muqaddas-i-Khurásán").
Piece 61 (the Báb's testamentary dispositions regarding his burial, partly translated by Browne in his Traveller's Narrative, vol. ii, p. 46 n. 1) is quoted by Browne in full.

Piece 64 contains a form of salutation to the sun, comparable to a passage Browne had translated from the Persian Bayán elsewhere (B. iv, pp. 929-930), which he notes as illustrating the Báb's revival of Zoroastrian ideas.
msItem.explicit: وان عند الله ام الکتاب به يمحو الله ما يشاء ثم يثبت ما يريد انه فعال لما يريد (piece 65, f. 199b)

listBibl.bibl: See sub classmark BBF. 3 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

msItem.textLang: Arabic, with some pieces or parts of pieces in Persian

handDesc.handNote: Written in Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel's clear and legible naskh hand.

history.origin: Place and date of transcription not stated by Browne.
history.provenance: Accompanied Ṣubḥ-i- Ezel's third letter to Browne, despatched from Famagusta, Cyprus, 3 December, received 18 December 1889.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: "Manuscript description based on. R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932. ", abbreviated by the OCIMCO project team; provenance and item notes expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábī Manuscripts," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Jul. 1892, pp. 470-483 (Browne's longest single entry in the catalogue; the c. 65 individual pieces are summarized here rather than each catalogued separately).

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBF_3.xml) into this Fihrist record: summary, item notes for the pieces Browne singles out as significant, incipit/explicit samples, physical description, hand note, and provenance history rewritten from Browne's 1892 JRAS catalogue account (pp. 470-483 - Browne's longest single entry, summarized rather than fully reproduced here as in the source draft). Arabic/Persian quotations are unproofed OCR (Tesseract), flagged cert="low". Person/place authority keys reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne, Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel, Cyprus). Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
Andrew Morrison Removed origDate tags around something which is not a date using fix-dates.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
